title: iF Design Award 2027
entryFee: From €400 per entry (regular, until Sep 18, 2026); €500 after
prizeMoney: iF Design Award trophy, seal, and global publicity (no cash prize)
shortDescription: One of the world's most prestigious design competitions, open to product, packaging, communication, interior, and UX design.
longDescription: The iF Design Award has run since 1954 and is judged by an independent panel of international experts across disciplines including product, packaging, communication, interior, and UX/UI design. Winning entries receive the iF seal, are published in the iF design app and yearbook, and are exhibited at iF's design centers in Hamburg and Taipei.
submissionFormat: Online entry with product images/renders; physical sample may be requested for the jury round

title: James Dyson Award 2026
shortDescription: An international student and recent-graduate award for engineering and product design that solves a real problem.
longDescription: Open to current and recent university students in engineering and design, the James Dyson Award rewards inventions that solve genuine, everyday problems. National winners are shortlisted before an international panel including James Dyson selects the global winner. The award emphasizes prototyping and iteration over polish.
submissionFormat: 1–2 A3 boards, a short process video, and supporting sketches submitted online

title: German Design Award 2027
entryFee: Free to register; participation fee applies once nominated
prizeMoney: German Design Award trophy and international recognition (no cash prize)
shortDescription: A cross-disciplinary award from the German Design Council, judged this cycle with a focus on communication design.
longDescription: Presented by the German Design Council, the German Design Award recognizes outstanding design work across product, communication, and architecture disciplines. Entrants must first be nominated before submitting; nominated work is judged by an independent jury of design experts, with winners announced at the awards show in Frankfurt.
submissionFormat: Online submission via the nomination platform: visuals plus a written project description

title: MUSE Design Awards 2026 (Season 2)
prizeMoney: MUSE trophy and global publicity (no cash prize)
shortDescription: A New York-based multidisciplinary design award spanning architecture, product, packaging, and graphic design.
longDescription: The MUSE Design Awards evaluate entries across dozens of subcategories, from architectural design to packaging and conceptual work. Judging is handled by a panel of design, media, and marketing professionals, and results include tiered Platinum, Gold, and Silver recognition rather than a single winner per category.
submissionFormat: Digital submission: images, a written description, and category-specific supporting material

title: A' Design Award & Competition 2026–2027
entryFee: Free to register; nomination fee applies only if shortlisted
prizeMoney: Trophy, gala presentation, and international press coverage
shortDescription: One of the largest global design competitions, covering over 100 categories including digital products and UX/UI.
longDescription: A' Design Award and Competition accepts entries across an unusually broad set of categories, including interface, interaction, and digital product design. Registration and initial submission are free; a nomination fee is only charged for entries that advance past preliminary review. Winners are announced annually on World Design Day, April 15.
submissionFormat: Digital submission: images, technical drawings, and a written design statement

title: Architecture MasterPrize 2026
entryFee: Tiered entry fee by deadline (early / regular / final pricing)
prizeMoney: Trophy and global publication (no cash prize)
shortDescription: An international prize covering architectural design, interiors, landscape architecture, and architectural products.
longDescription: The Architecture MasterPrize invites built and unbuilt work from architects, interior designers, and landscape architects worldwide. Past editions have drawn entries from more than 70 countries. Winners are announced roughly a month after the final submission deadline and are featured in the organizer's annual publication.
submissionFormat: Digital submission: drawings, renders, photographs, and a project narrative

title: Interior Design Best of Year Awards 2026
organizer: SANDOW Design Group / Interior Design magazine
prizeMoney: Trophy and industry-wide recognition (no cash prize)
shortDescription: A long-running US interiors award covering completed projects and furniture/product design across many typologies.
longDescription: Interior Design's Best of Year Awards honor standout interiors and the products that furnish them, judged by a panel of practicing designers and editors. Categories span workplace, hospitality, residential, and healthcare interiors alongside furniture, lighting, and materials, with winners celebrated at an in-person ceremony.
submissionFormat: Digital submission: project or product photography, plans, and a written narrative

title: Bundespreis Ecodesign 2026
organizer: German Federal Ministry for the Environment / Umweltbundesamt
entryFee: Fee scales by company size; free for the student/newcomer (Nachwuchs) category
prizeMoney: Recognition award plus PR and communications support (no cash prize)
shortDescription: Germany's federal design award for products, services, and concepts with strong ecological performance.
longDescription: Presented by the German Federal Ministry for the Environment and the Umweltbundesamt, the Bundespreis Ecodesign recognizes companies, studios, and researchers whose work combines ecological responsibility with high design quality. A dedicated Nachwuchs (newcomer) category is open to students and graduates who finished their degree within the last year. This cycle's application window has closed; the entry remains listed for reference ahead of the next call.
submissionFormat: Online application with product/service documentation and ecological impact data

title: Green Product Award 2027
organizer: Green Product Award / Green Future Club
entryFee: Tiered entry fee (early bird / regular / final pricing)
prizeMoney: Trophy, certification seal, and audience award (no cash prize)
shortDescription: An international award for sustainable products and services across 14 categories, from architecture to mobility.
longDescription: The Green Product Award has recognized sustainable products and services since 2012, inviting companies and start-ups to submit work across categories spanning architecture, consumer goods, mobility, and new materials. Nominees are published for public audience voting ahead of the final jury decision, with the award ceremony held in Berlin.
submissionFormat: Online submission: product images, a sustainability data sheet, and a written description

title: International Design Awards (IDA) 2026
entryFee: ~$250 per entry (professionals); ~$80 per entry (students), with early discounts
prizeMoney: Gold, Silver, and Bronze recognition plus press coverage (no cash prize)
shortDescription: A long-running Los Angeles-based award spanning architecture, interior, product, fashion, and graphic design.
longDescription: The International Design Awards recognize both professionals and students across five core disciplines: architecture, interior, product, fashion, and graphic design. Entries must have been completed within the last five years. Winners are graded Gold, Silver, or Bronze within each category, with top honorees also considered for Designer of the Year and Emerging Designer of the Year.
submissionFormat: Digital submission: images, technical details, and a project statement

title: Green Good Design Award 2027
organizer: The Chicago Athenaeum: Museum of Architecture and Design
entryFee: €375 per entry (+€25 if paying by bank transfer); +€100 late fee
prizeMoney: Trophy, exhibition, and international recognition (no cash prize)
shortDescription: An international award for products, graphics, and organizations with strong ecological and sustainability credentials, presented by The Chicago Athenaeum.
longDescription: Part of the Good Design Award family administered by The Chicago Athenaeum and The European Centre for Architecture Art Design and Urban Studies, the Green Good Design Award recognizes work across six category groups: architecture, graphic design, organizations & people, product design, research & technology, and transportation. Winners are exhibited and published as part of the Good Design Awards program.
submissionFormat: Online submission: 4-6 project images (JPG/PNG, 300dpi) plus a written project description (max 500 words)

title: Communication Arts Typography Competition 2027
entryFee: From $40 per entry (varies by category); +$10 late fee after Sept 4, 2026; student discount available
prizeMoney: Publication in the Communication Arts Typography Annual (no cash prize)
shortDescription: One of the most prestigious competitions for typographic excellence, covering hand-lettering, calligraphy, and type design.
longDescription: Selected by a jury of distinguished designers and art directors, winning entries in the Communication Arts Typography Competition are published in the Typography Annual, included in the magazine's January/February issue and distributed worldwide. Entrants are notified within roughly two months of the deadline; entry fees vary by category with a discount for student submissions.
submissionFormat: Digital upload of high-resolution images or PDF documents showing the typographic work in context
